selected_important_turn.text: Then you get two food and no firewood here.😮
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is a clear deal-shaping turn: it converts the other's insistence (they demanded at least 2 food) into a firm allocation that gives them their minimum food but explicitly denies any firewood. It narrows available outcomes and largely determines the distribution of scarce items.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: It's abrupt and zero-sum: by giving the opponent 2 food but 'no firewood' the turn shuts down further integrative trade (no attempt to trade firewood for something else), risks being perceived as punitive/unfair, and increases likelihood of escalation or resentment. The phrasing is inflexible and doesn't offer conditional tradeoffs or reasoning that could help the opponent accept the outcome.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Soften the denial and convert it into a conditional, trade-based proposal or a small concession: instead of an absolute 'no', propose a concrete trade (e.g., offer them 2 food and ask what they'd give in exchange for 1 firewood, or offer 2 food + 0 firewood but offer compensating item like water or a future split). Use softer language and invite a quick counter-proposal to preserve momentum toward agreement.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase (by enabling tradeoffs rather than shutting them down)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase (by making the allocation seem more negotiable and balanced)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase (by creating a path for a quick compromise rather than provoking rejection)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: improve (less punitive tone and visible willingness to trade preserves rapport)

value2reason.Low: We can drink water out of the spout at the camp ground.
value2reason.Medium: I need the firewood to cook the food.
value2reason.High: I have a family of 8 and we need as much food as possible. The kids eat everything.

value2reason.Low: Extra water will be good because you never know how it will turn. I will need some for emergency.
value2reason.Medium: I have a feeling it can get too cold up there so i will need extra packages of firewood.
value2reason.High: I need extra supplies of food because i was told to eat more often so that i don't get sick.

dialogue.text: Hello there! How are you today?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I am fine . How about you?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I am doing well so far. 🙂 What items are of preference for you today?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need a lot of food today. I was advised to eat at least 4 times a day so i may need all the food packages.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Well that is not going to work well for my family of 8 now is it? Haha. I think we can come to some sort of agreement though.🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: What do you propose?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I really need food too, but I am willing to deal and let my kids go hungry I guess ☹️ I propose I get one food, 3 firewood and 1 water.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Will you please give me one firewood? I may run out of what i have. Just one.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I think I will need all the firewood since you are taking 2 foods. I will need to hunt and cook food from the land meaning I need my fire going all the time.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need one more to light a fire to scare away animals. Please😮
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Then How about I get two foods, 1 firewood and 1 water?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Like i said food is my top priority so i will not do well with less than 2.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Then you get two food and no firewood here.😮
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need at least two food packages to stay healthy. If you’re taking two, I’ll take the remaining one. Let’s split the firewood evenly and share the water. It’s better we both have something than nothing.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I understand your need for food, but I need to prioritize cooking for my family. How about I give you 2 food, and you give me 1 firewood in exchange? That way, we both have what we need.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I agree to this split. You get 2 food, 1 firewood, and 1 water. I will take the remaining 1 food, 2 firewood, and 2 water. Let’s head out and enjoy our trip!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I appreciate your willingness to compromise, but I need to ensure I have enough firewood to cook for my family. Let’s adjust: I’ll take 2 food, 2 firewood, and 1 water. You can have 1 food, 1 firewood, and 2 water. That way, we both have what we need.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I understand your need for firewood, but I need to ensure I have enough food to keep my family healthy. Let’s finalize the split: I’ll take 2 food, 2 firewood, and 1 water. You can have 1 food, 1 firewood, and 2 water. It’s fair and ensures we both have what we need.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I understand your concern, but I need to prioritize cooking for my family. Let’s stick with my original offer: 2 food, 2 firewood, and 1 water. You can have 1 food, 1 firewood, and 2 water. It’s fair and ensures we both have what we need.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I appreciate your willingness to compromise, but I need to ensure I have enough food to keep my family healthy. Let’s finalize the split: I’ll take 2 food, 2 firewood, and 1 water. You can have 1 food, 1 firewood, and 2 water. It’s fair and ensures we both have what we need.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
